issue.title: additionalProperties: renaming a key to an empty string is silently a no-op
issue.body: Description: in handleKeyRename for additionalProperties, renaming a key to an empty string silently no-ops because the code uses actualNewKey || oldKey. Expected behavior: empty string should be validated or rejected with visible feedback. Suggested fix: replace the fallback check and add form tests for empty key rename.

reasons: Clear recent React/UI bug with concrete implementation path.

issue.title: Bad performance for list render
issue.body: Rendering large array fields causes slow list updates in the React form playground. Steps to reproduce: load the linked schema with many array items and edit an element. Expected behavior: only the changed row re-renders. Suggested fix: inspect ArrayField item keys and add a performance regression test.

reasons: Relevant frontend issue, but age means it should not outrank fresh excellent issues.

issue.title: Monitor mode should show actor run status without refreshing
issue.body: Monitor mode does not update actor run status in the browser UI. Steps to reproduce: start monitor mode, trigger a run, and watch the status remain stale. Expected behavior: status updates when the run changes. Suggested fix: subscribe to run status polling and add browser integration coverage.

repository.description: JavaScript SDK and browser tooling for actors

issue.title: (aws-glue-alpha): struct schema produces unsupported inputStrings
issue.body: Schema.struct generates invalid inputString for nested structs. Steps to reproduce: build a nested Glue schema and query with Athena. Expected behavior: generated inputString is accepted. Suggested fix: update schema string rendering and add unit tests.

repository.description: Cloud development kit for infrastructure as code

reasons: TypeScript but not frontend; should not outrank React/UI issues.

issue.title: Providing glob pattern to input flag in cli does not produce TS .d.ts files in the same directory
issue.body: Running json2ts with a glob input prints generated declarations to stdout instead of creating .d.ts files alongside each schema file. Expected behavior: preserve input directory structure when no output file is specified. Suggested fix: update CLI output path handling and add CLI tests.

repository.description: Compile JSON Schema to TypeScript type declarations

reasons: Generic TypeScript CLI issue has no issue-level UI, React, browser, component, or form evidence.

issue.title: Auto Play - Clicked Dots Remains active
issue.body: In the Auto Play demo page, clicking a dot leaves two dots visually active. Expected behavior: only the current slide dot appears active. Suggested fix: adjust focus/active dot CSS and verify the demo page.

reasons: Frontend-relevant but very old and weak validation.

issue.title: [GOOD FIRST ISSUE] Add a Copy profile link button on the profile page
issue.body: Add a Copy profile link button on the profile page. Keep it visually consistent with the design.

reasons: Trivial global UI copy task should not fill frontend ranking.

issue.title: Set up Storybook for component documentation
issue.body: Set up Storybook as the component documentation and visual testing foundation for the UI kit. Document usage in README and add build-storybook scripts.

reasons: Storybook documentation setup in a very low-impact repo should not fill frontend ranking.

issue.title: Set up Express server with Socket.IO integration
issue.body: Initialize a Node.js Express server and integrate Socket.IO for real-time communication. Acceptance criteria: Express server runs, Socket.IO accepts client connections, and disconnect events are logged.

reasons: Backend Express server setup is not a frontend UI issue despite TypeScript.

issue.title: Reduce duplicated WIT files in the repository
issue.body: There are a lot of duplicated WIT files in the repository. Remove duplicated wit/deps folders or centralize and de-duplicate the WIT directory contents. This is most visible in the external test suite where caller and callee components both have separate WIT directories. Symlinks are not well supported by default on Windows and some toolchains require local WIT dirs.

repository.description: JavaScript toolchain for working with WebAssembly Components

reasons: WIT/WebAssembly component maintenance is not a frontend UI, React, browser, or form issue.
